etymology.description: 燕(燕) (yàn, "swallow" [the bird]) is a sound-loan here, borrowed only for its sound, not its meaning. 麥(麥) (mài, "wheat") represents grains in general. 粥(粥) (zhōu, "porridge") comes from the concept of grain boiled into a soft, consumable form."
etymology.interpretation: 燕(燕) (yàn, "swallow" [the bird]) was borrowed for its phonetic value and reflects no semantic contribution to the meaning. 麥(麥) (mài, "wheat") signifies cereal grains. Together, 燕麦(燕麥) (yànmài, "oats") designates a specific type of cereal grain, differentiated from wheat (麦). 粥(粥) (zhōu, "porridge") refers to the preparation method of grains being cooked into a thick, soft dish suitable for eating. Thus, 燕麦粥(燕麥粥) (yànmàizhōu, "oatmeal porridge") logically denotes a porridge made from oats, emphasizing the grain used and its transformation into a consumable form.

examples.chinese: 我每天早餐都吃燕麦粥(我每天早餐都吃燕麥粥)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān zǎocān dōu chī yànmàizhōu.
examples.english: I eat oatmeal porridge for breakfast every day.

examples.chinese: 燕麦粥对健康有益(燕麥粥對健康有益)。
examples.english: Oatmeal porridge is beneficial to health.

examples.chinese: 她在炉子上煮了一锅燕麦粥(她在爐子上煮了一鍋燕麥粥)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài lúzi shàng zhǔ le yī guō yànmàizhōu.
examples.english: She cooked a pot of oatmeal porridge on the stove.
